title: rubus lupimontanus
intro:
Rubus lupimontanus, also known as the Alpine Raspberry or Alpine Bramble, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its edible fruits and medicinal properties.

The plant contains anthocyanins, which have antioxidant properties, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Rubus lupimontanus is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Rubus lupimontanus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
